req.title: Bill pay
req.statement: A customer shall pay a registered biller between $1 and $50,000 with deterministic settlement date calculation based on biller cut-off.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines a bill-pay endpoint with: (1) a valid amount range [$1, $50000], (2) deterministic settlement date based on biller cut-off, and (3) a 422 error for out-of-range amounts. Test design applies EP to confirm a mid-range amount succeeds, BVA to probe the exact lower ($1) and upper ($50000) boundaries as well as the just-inside-upper boundary ($49999.99), NEG to confirm amounts outside the range are rejected with 422 (using clearly invalid out-of-range values not tied to BVA boundary probing), DT to verify settlement-date logic varies deterministically by biller cut-off flag, and STC to confirm the resource transitions from 'pending' to 'scheduled' after a successful post. The violation fix for test[4] changes kind from 'negative' to 'boundary' because $50000.01 is the BVA just-above-upper boundary point — it is a boundary probe, not a generic negative test. A separate NEG test using a clearly non-boundary invalid amount (e.g., $99999.99) is retained to cover the negative/error pathway partition independently.

tests.name: test_billpay_valid_mid_range_amount_returns_scheduled
preconditions: Biller with id <BILLER_ID> is registered in the system
expected: Response body contains a non-null settlement_date
trace: R-FIN-007: payment scheduled; settlement date returned
EP: 500.00 is a representative value from the valid partition [$1, $50000]

tests.name: test_billpay_amount_below_minimum_returns_422
preconditions: Biller with id <BILLER_ID> is registered in the system
expected: Response body contains an error referencing amount out of range
trace: R-FIN-007: 422 when amount out of range
NEG: 0.99 is one cent below the $1 lower bound — invalid partition

expected: Response body contains a non-null settlement_date

expected: Response body contains a non-null settlement_date

BVA: $50000.00 is the exact upper boundary of the valid amount range

tests.name: test_billpay_amount_just_above_maximum_boundary_returns_422
preconditions: Biller with id <BILLER_ID> is registered in the system
expected: Response body contains an error referencing amount out of range
trace: R-FIN-007: 422 when amount out of range
BVA: $50000.01 is the first value just above the $50000 upper boundary — tests the boundary transition from valid to invalid partition

tests.name: test_billpay_amount_well_above_maximum_returns_422
preconditions: Biller with id <BILLER_ID> is registered in the system
expected: Response body contains an error referencing amount out of range
trace: R-FIN-007: 422 when amount out of range
NEG: $99999.99 is well above the $50000 upper bound — representative of the invalid upper partition, distinct from the BVA boundary probe

tests.name: test_billpay_settlement_date_differs_by_cutoff_flag
preconditions: Biller A (<BILLER_BEFORE_CUTOFF_ID>) has cut-off flag set to before_cutoff=true
Biller B (<BILLER_AFTER_CUTOFF_ID>) has cut-off flag set to before_cutoff=false
Requests are submitted at the same logical time (mocked or controlled clock)
expected: settlement_date for biller_before_cutoff response is earlier than settlement_date for biller_after_cutoff response
trace: R-FIN-007: deterministic settlement date calculation based on biller cut-off
DT: decision table row 1 — before_cutoff=true yields earlier settlement date; row 2 — before_cutoff=false yields later settlement date
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import date

BASE_URL = "<BASE_URL>"

@pytest.fixture
def auth_headers():
    return <auth_headers_fixture>

def test_billpay_settlement_date_differs_by_cutoff_flag(auth_headers):
    payload_before = {
        "biller_id": "<BILLER_BEFORE_CUTOFF_ID>",
        "account_id": "<ACCOUNT_ID>",
        "amount": 100.00,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    payload_after = {
        "biller_id": "<BILLER_AFTER_CUTOFF_ID>",
        "account_id": "<ACCOUNT_ID>",
        "amount": 100.00,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp_before = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/billpay", json=payload_before, headers=auth_headers)
    resp_after = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/billpay", json=payload_after, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp_before.status_code == 201
    assert resp_after.status_code == 201
    body_before = resp_before.json()
    body_after = resp_after.json()
    assert body_before["status"] == "scheduled"
    assert body_after["status"] == "scheduled"
    settlement_before = date.fromisoformat(body_before["settlement_date"])
    settlement_after = date.fromisoformat(body_after["settlement_date"])
    assert settlement_before < settlement_after

tests.name: test_billpay_status_transitions_to_scheduled_after_post
preconditions: Biller with id <BILLER_ID> is registered in the system
actions: GET /billpay/<payment_id> using the id returned in the POST response
expected: GET response body contains the same settlement_date as the POST response
trace: R-FIN-007: payment scheduled; settlement date returned
STC: state transition — initial POST must produce 'scheduled' state; subsequent GET must confirm persisted 'scheduled' state
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "<BASE_URL>"

@pytest.fixture
def auth_headers():
    return <auth_headers_fixture>

@pytest.fixture
def registered_biller():
    return <registered_biller_fixture>

def test_billpay_status_transitions_to_scheduled_after_post(auth_headers, registered_biller):
    payload = {
        "biller_id": registered_biller["id"],
        "account_id": "<ACCOUNT_ID>",
        "amount": 200.00,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    post_resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/billpay", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert post_resp.status_code == 201
    post_body = post_resp.json()
    assert post_body["status"] == "scheduled"
    payment_id = post_body["id"]
    settlement_date_from_post = post_body["settlement_date"]
    get_resp = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/billpay/{payment_id}", headers=auth_headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    get_body = get_resp.json()
    assert get_body["status"] == "scheduled"
    assert get_body["settlement_date"] == settlement_date_from_post
